subject: Sociology
title: Time-Space Compression
description: A micro-lesson that makes Time-Space Compression usable.

hook.text: A letter once took months to cross the ocean. Now a video call connects continents in milliseconds.

buildup.text: David Harvey coined time-space compression: technology shrinks the effective distance between places and people.

discovery.text: Jet travel, internet, and phones make global communication instant — collapsing barriers that shaped history.

twist.text: Compression isn't equal. Rural villages may lack internet while urban elites video-call across hemispheres.

climax.text: Time-space compression reorganizes social life. Where you are matters less; what you can access matters more.

punchline.text: Distance shrank, but not for everyone.

quiz.question: What is time-space compression?
options: Technology shrinking effective distance between places
